message:
br-bd-yuhsu FIBMAP ioctl: legacy logical->physical block lookup via fiemap

filefrag -B, e2fsck, and e2image still depend on the legacy FIBMAP
ioctl (FIBMAP = _IO(0, 1) = 0x1) to ask the FS for the physical
block number of a single logical file block. ext4 implements it via
ext4_get_block which traverses the extent tree; FrankenFS already
has ffs_extent::map_logical_to_physical exposed through
FsOps::fiemap, so FIBMAP becomes a thin wrapper that:

- parses the 4-byte u32 logical-block input,
- pulls the FS block size from statfs (fallback 4096 if statfs fails),
- runs ops.fiemap over a 1-block window starting at
  logical * block_size,
- picks the extent that actually covers the queried logical block
  (fiemap may return the next-following extent if the query falls
  in a hole),
- returns the matching physical block number — or 0 for a hole, per
  fs/ext4/inode.c::ext4_get_block.

Tests: 695 ffs-core + 266 ffs-fuse pass. New regressions:
- ffs-fuse: dispatch_ioctl_fibmap_short_input_returns_einval (3-byte
  in_data -> EINVAL).
- ffs-fuse: dispatch_ioctl_fibmap_short_output_returns_einval (3-byte
  out_size -> EINVAL).
- ffs-core: ext4_fibmap_logical_block_matches_fiemap_for_written_data
  asserts the 4 KiB at offset 0 has a non-zero physical block visible
  through the same code path FIBMAP uses.
- ffs-core: ext4_fibmap_logical_block_in_hole_returns_zero asserts an
  unwritten file's logical block 0 maps to physical 0 (FIBMAP-encoded
  hole sentinel).

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_yuhsu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
